Headers.Name: Authorization
Headers.Description: Authentication information, refer to API authentication

Body.Name: video_id
Body.Description: The ID of the video generated by the Kling AI
Body.Notes:
Only videos generated within the last 30 days are supported


Only supports videos with a duration of ≥2 seconds and ≤5 seconds, or ≥7 seconds and ≤10 seconds


Related to the video_url parameter: both video_id and video_url cannot be empty at the same time, and cannot both have values at the same time

Body.Name: video_url
Body.Notes:
Only .mp4/.mov formats are supported


Only supports videos with a duration of ≥2 seconds and ≤5 seconds, or ≥7 seconds and ≤10 seconds


Video resolution must be between 720px and 2160px (inclusive) in both width and height


Only supports videos with frame rates of 24, 30, or 60 fps


Related to the video_id parameter: both video_id and video_url cannot be empty at the same time, and cannot both have values at the same time

Comments.code: // Error code; Specific definitions can be found in "Error Code"
Comments.data.fps: // Frame rate of parsed video, required when fetching selection preview video
Comments.data.height: // Height of parsed video, currently unused
Comments.data.original_duration: // Duration of parsed video, required when creating task
Comments.data.session_id: // Session ID, generated during video initialization task, remains unchanged during editing operations, valid for 24 hours
Comments.data.status: // Rejection code, non-zero indicates recognition failure
Comments.data.total_frame: // Total frame count of parsed video, required when creating task
Comments.data.width: // Width of parsed video, currently unused
Comments.request_id: // Request ID, generated by the system, used for tracking requests and troubleshooting

Headers.Name: Authorization
Headers.Description: Authentication information, refer to API authentication

Body.Name: session_id
Body.Description: Session ID, generated during the video initialization task and remains unchanged during editing operations

Body.Name: frame_index
Body.Notes:
A maximum of 10 frames can be marked. That is, up to 10 frames can be used to define selection areas in the video


Only supports marking 1 frame at a time

Body.Name: points
Body.Description: Click Coordinates, represented by x and y
Body.Notes:
Value range: [0, 1], expressed as percentages; [0, 1] represents the top-left corner of the frame


Multiple points can be marked at once; up to 10 points can be marked on a single frame

Comments.code: // Error code; Specific definitions can be found in "Error Code"
Comments.data.session_id: // Session ID, generated during video initialization task, remains unchanged during editing operations, valid for 24 hours
Comments.data.status: // Rejection code, non-zero indicates recognition failure
Comments.request_id: // Request ID, generated by the system, used for tracking requests and troubleshooting

Body.Name: points
Body.Description: Click Coordinates to delete, represented by x and y
Body.Notes:
Value range: [0, 1], expressed as percentages; [0, 1] represents the top-left corner of the frame


Multiple points can be provided at once


Coordinates must exactly match those used when adding the video selection area

Req.Code:
curl --request POST \
  --url https://api-singapore.klingai.com/v1/videos/multi-elements \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data '{
    "model_name": "kling-v1-6",
    "session_id": "847570360458960960",
    "edit_mode": "removal",
    "image_list": [],
    "prompt": "Delete the chick from <<<video_1>>>",
    "negative_prompt": "",
    "mode": "std",
    "duration": "5",
    "callback_url": "",
    "external_task_id": ""
  }'

Headers.Name: Authorization
Headers.Description: Authentication information, refer to API authentication

Body.Name: session_id
Body.Description: Session ID, generated during the video initialization task and remains unchanged during editing operations

Body.Name: edit_mode
Body.Notes:
addition: Add an element


swap: Replace an element


removal: Remove an element

Body.Name: image_list
Body.Notes:
For adding video elements: This parameter is required; upload 1–2 images


For editing (swapping) video elements: This parameter is required; upload 1 image only


For deleting video elements: This parameter is not required


Use key:value format as follows:

"image_list":[
  { "image":"image_url" },
  { "image":"image_url" }
]













The API does not perform cropping, please upload images with subjects already cropped


Supports image input as either Base64-encoded string or URL (ensure the URL is publicly accessible)


Important: When using Base64, do NOT add any prefix like data:image/png;base64,. Submit only the raw Base64 string.


Correct Base64 format:

iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAUA...













Incorrect Base64 format (with data: prefix):

data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAUA...













Supported image formats: .jpg / .jpeg / .png


Image file size must not exceed 10MB. Image dimensions must be at least 300px. Aspect ratio must be between 1:2.5 and 2.5:1

Body.Name: prompt
Body.Notes:
Use the format <<<xxx>>> to explicitly refer to a specific video or image, such as <<<video_1>>> or <<<image_1>>>


To ensure optimal results, the prompt must include references to the video and image(s) required for the editing


Must not exceed 2,500 characters


💡
Recommended Prompt Templates:
Adding Elements:
EN: Using the context of <<<video_1>>>, seamlessly add [x] from <<<image_1>>>
ZH: 基于<<<video_1>>>中的原始内容，以自然生动的方式，将<<<image_1>>>中的【】，融入<<<video_1>>>的【】
Replacing Elements:
EN: swap [x] from <<<image_1>>> for [x] from <<<video_1>>>
ZH: 使用<<<image_1>>>中的【】，替换<<<video_1>>>中的【】
Removing Elements:
EN: Delete [x] from <<<video_1>>>
ZH: 删除<<<video_1>>>中的【】
Note: [x] or 【】 are placeholders where you should fill in specific content.

Body.Name: mode
Body.Notes:
std: Standard mode, basic rendering, cost-effective


pro: Professional mode, high-quality, enhanced rendering, better video output quality

Body.Name: duration
Body.Notes:
Only 5-second and 10-second videos are supported


To generate a 5-second video, the input video must be ≥2 seconds and ≤5 seconds


To generate a 10-second video, the input video must be ≥7 seconds and ≤10 seconds

Body.Name: watermark_info
Body.Description: Whether to generate watermarked results simultaneously
Body.Notes:
Defined by the enabled parameter, format:

  "watermark_info": { "enabled": boolean } 













true: generate watermarked result, false: do not generate


Custom watermarks are not currently supported

Body.Name: callback_url
Body.Description: Callback URL for Task Result Notification. If configured, the server will actively send notifications when the task status changes
Body.Notes: For the message schema, refer to the Callback Protocol

Body.Name: external_task_id
Body.Notes:
A user-defined task ID; it will not overwrite the system-generated task ID, but can be used to query the task


Please ensure uniqueness of the task ID within a single user account

Comments.code: // Error code; Specific definitions can be found in "Error Code"
Comments.data.created_at: // Task creation time, Unix timestamp, unit: ms
Comments.data.task_id: // Task ID, generated by the system
Comments.data.task_status: // Task status, Enum values: submitted, processing, succeed, failed
Comments.data.updated_at: // Task update time, Unix timestamp, unit: ms
Comments.request_id: // Request ID, generated by the system, used for tracking requests and troubleshooting

PathParams.Name: task_id
PathParams.Description: Task ID for Multi-Elements video editing
PathParams.Notes:
Request path parameter, fill the value directly in the request path


You can choose to query by external_task_id or task_id

PathParams.Name: external_task_id
PathParams.Description: Custom Task ID for Multi-Elements video editing
PathParams.Notes: The external_task_id filled in when creating the task. You can choose to query by external_task_id or task_id

Headers.Name: Authorization
Headers.Description: Authentication information, refer to API authentication

Comments.code: // Error code; Specific definitions can be found in "Error Code"
Comments.data.created_at: // Task creation time, Unix timestamp, unit: ms
Comments.data.final_unit_deduction: // Final unit deduction for the task
Comments.data.task_id: // Task ID, generated by the system
Comments.data.task_result.videos.session_id: // Session ID, generated during video initialization task, remains unchanged during editing operations, valid for 24 hours
Comments.data.task_result.videos.url: // URL of generated video (Note: For security purposes, generated images/videos will be deleted after 30 days, please save them promptly)
Comments.data.task_result.videos.watermark_url: // Watermarked video download URL, anti-hotlinking format
Comments.data.task_status: // Task status, Enum values: submitted, processing, succeed, failed
Comments.data.task_status_msg: // Task status message, displays failure reason when task fails (e.g., triggered platform content moderation)
Comments.data.updated_at: // Task update time, Unix timestamp, unit: ms
Comments.request_id: // Request ID, generated by the system, used for tracking requests and troubleshooting
